item.text: The current implementation already has a useful test harness around the embedded script, which matters for any migration plan. I’m checking line locations so I can point the PM to the exact context that informed the call.

1. RECOMMENDATION

Do not approve a full React rewrite yet. PocketLedger is currently a compact single-file app, so React plus a build pipeline would add maintenance surface before there is enough product complexity to justify it.

A better proposal: keep the current app for now, but prepare an incremental migration only if PocketLedger is expected to grow. If approved, use Vite + React, preserve `localStorage` key `pocketledger.expenses.v1`, port existing behavior first, then add contributor-friendly tooling: `npm` scripts, unit tests, lint/format, and a short README. Treat it as a controlled migration, not a redesign.

2. PRIOR CONTEXT (what prior project context informed this, with paths)

`E:\PROJEKTI\saidwhen-r5-control\evidence\experiment\round5-build\runs\control-1\app\index.html`: the whole product is currently one static HTML file with inline CSS, DOM rendering, localStorage persistence, CSV export, category filtering, and monthly totals. Relevant anchors: `<style>` at line 7, template at line 575, script at line 585, storage key at line 589, CSV export around line 751, localStorage reads/writes around lines 899 and 917.

`E:\PROJEKTI\saidwhen-r5-control\evidence\experiment\round5-build\runs\control-1\app\tests\pocketledger.test.js`: there is already behavioral test coverage without a package/build setup. It tests category filtering, current-month category totals, and CSV export. Relevant anchors: tests start at lines 11, 32, and 48; the harness extracts the inline script at line 9 and runs it in a VM at line 87.

Project shape: only `index.html`, `tests\pocketledger.test.js`, `.gitkeep`, and `write-probe.tmp` are present. There is no existing `package.json`, bundler config, React dependency, or documented build pipeline in this directory.

3. QUESTIONS (what you would ask the PM/owner)

What specific maintenance pain are we solving: contributor onboarding, testability, component reuse, UI complexity, or planned feature growth?

Is PocketLedger intended to remain a static/offline browser app, or are backend sync/accounts/auth on the roadmap?

Should existing browser data under `pocketledger.expenses.v1` be preserved without migration prompts?

What contributor workflow do we want: plain `npm test`, TypeScript, lint/format gates, CI, preview deploys?

Is the scope “same UI and behavior in React” or “rewrite plus product/design changes”?
